title: LSASS Credential Dump
briefing: Sysmon on $TARGET recorded a process dumping the memory of LSASS via comsvcs.dll MiniDump (rundll32 ... comsvcs.dll, MiniDump ... lsass.dmp). LSASS holds cached credentials and tickets — dumping it is how attackers harvest secrets for lateral movement. Confirm the host and the technique.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100143. Read data.win.eventdata.commandLine for comsvcs.dll MiniDump / lsass.dmp.

questions.explain: rundll32 calling comsvcs.dll MiniDump against LSASS writes a memory dump full of credentials.

questions.prompt: Which command-line artefact is the smoking gun?
questions.explain: comsvcs.dll MiniDump is a well-known LOLBin technique to dump LSASS without external tools.

questions.prompt: Which host was the credential dump run on?

questions.explain: T1003.001 OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory.
A successful LSASS dump means every credential cached on that host is compromised; treat as critical.

options: Force reset of every account that logged on to it (incl. privileged)
Hunt for lateral movement from the host
Wait and see if anything else happens
answer: Force reset of every account that logged on to it (incl. privileged)
Hunt for lateral movement from the host
questions.explain: Isolate, rotate all exposed creds, hunt outward, and harden LSASS so it cannot be dumped again.
